question: How many weeks did it take me to watch all the Marvel Cinematic Universe movies and the main Star Wars films?

retrieval_results.context:
User recently completed a Star Wars marathon, watching all the main films in a week and a half, describing the experience as a wild ride, and intends to add these films to their movie list.
User is attempting to organize their movie watching history and has recently completed an intense binge of all 22 Marvel Cinematic Universe movies in just two weeks, indicating a strong interest in superhero franchises and a capacity for rapid consumption.
User also watched Rogue One and Solo, and plans to add these Star Wars spin‑off films to their list.
User wants to add additional movies such as Captain Marvel, Us, Shazam!, and A Quiet Place to their list.
User is building a spreadsheet-based movie tracking system, adding columns for title, release year, watch date, rating, notes, franchise/series, genre, type, platform, format, re‑watch status, month watched, and optional columns such as director, cast, runtime, and language, and prefers bulk‑entering data via copy‑paste, feeling overwhelmed by entering many movies.
User has also watched Spider‑Man: Into the Spider‑Verse, Crazy Rich Asians, 2001: A Space Odyssey, The Shawshank Redemption, Jurassic Park, Toy Story 4, Midsommar, and The Lion King remake, and plans to include them in their movie tracker.
User has added a 'Month Watched' column to their spreadsheet to monitor monthly viewing patterns and analyze what movies were watched each month.
User has watched Game of Thrones season 1 in its entirety and is currently watching season 5 of The Office, and intends to add these TV shows to their tracking list.
User chooses to use Method 1—copying and pasting a pre-written list of movies from a note-taking app into the spreadsheet—to streamline data entry.
User intends to use the spreadsheet system to track future movies.
